scope_note: QA Scott T-Transformer cert [308]. Primary sources: Hardy, G.H. and Wright, E.M. (2008) An Introduction to the Theory of Numbers, Oxford, ISBN 978-0-19-921986-5; Serre, J.-P. (1973) A Course in Arithmetic, Springer, ISBN 978-0-387-90041-1; Chapman, S.J. (2011) Electric Machinery Fundamentals, 5th ed., McGraw-Hill, ISBN 978-0-07-352954-7. CLAIM: (C1) 3-phase subgroup {0,8,16} leq Z/24Z: M^8 has order 3 (M^8 neq I, M^16 neq I, M^24=I mod 24); 8 T-steps=120deg equivalent in 24-step orbit. (C2) 2-phase subgroup {0,6,12,18} leq Z/24Z: M^6 has order 4 (M^6,M^12,M^18 neq I, M^24=I mod 24); 6 T-steps=90deg equivalent. (C3) Scott-T SL(2,Z) word: W=R^{-1}.L^{-1}=[[2,-1],[-1,1]]=M^{-2}; M^2.W=I (exact); M^8.W=M^6 mod 24; unique length-2 SL(2,Z) correction bridging 3-phase step to 2-phase step (offset 8-6=2). (C4) Rational Scott-T coefficient: direction spread s(T^6(1,1),T^8(1,1))=289/1250 (exact Fraction); T^6=(4,3), T^8=(7,1); s=(4*1-3*7)^2/((25)*(50))=289/1250; rational-trig analog of physical sqrt(3)/2 transformer ratio. (C5) Theorem NT: sqrt(3)/2 approx 0.866 is observer projection; Scott-T transform in QA=group operation W=R^{-1}L^{-1} on Z/24Z orbit clock; no transcendental arithmetic in QA layer. Builds on cert [291] (M^24=I), cert [294] (SL(2,Z) spine L,R), cert [296] (M^2=L.R versor isomorphism), cert [303] (3-phase=3I). Lineage: derived 2026-06-02.
